texto: Actually, Elizabeth realized, when he laughed like that, he did look quite handsome. His dark brown hair and his eyes, so light a brown as to seem almost gold in color, were not quite as nondescript as she'd once thought. Maybe the giggling young ingenues weren't completely daft. Despite his maddening tendencies to be deliberately obtuse and tease her relentlessly, he had a certain charisma.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Emma Wildes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “Actually, Elizabeth realized, when he laughed like that, he did look quite handsome. His dark bro…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, charisma, first pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao-noturna.

Despite his maddening tendencies to be deliberately obtuse and tease her relentlessly, he had a certain charisma.

texto: The most impoverished peasant can be delighted by the opening of the first spring flower, and the most wealthy aristocrat can curse the day he was born because of some petty offense to his sensibilities. She is a very wise woman. To achieve serenity we have to view life not as it is measured by the world around us but as we ourselves measure it. We must accept that the scales are not at all equal.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Emma Wildes sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “The most impoverished peasant can be delighted by the opening of the first spring flower, and the…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sucesso, poor, rich, útil em momentos de rich e social-classes.

texto: The implied trust was humbling. He didn't deserve it, but then again, he wouldn't betray it either.
explicacao: Citação de Emma Wildes sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The implied trust was humbling. He didn't deserve it, but then again, he wouldn't betray it either.” articula reflexao, betrayals, deserve em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como deserve e humbling.
